issuing_body: Garante per la protezione dei dati personali
rights_terms_note: Public regulator page; no bulk mirroring assessed this cycle; quote sparingly and preserve Italian original for legal nuance.
cautious_summary: The Italian DPA ordered an urgent provisional limitation on OpenAI's processing of personal data of data subjects in Italy via ChatGPT, citing lack of information to users/data subjects, lack of suitable legal basis for training-related processing, accuracy concerns, and absence of age verification in the order text.
observatory_hook: Feeds regulator-action timeline, jurisdiction map, and privacy/data-protection enforcement matrix for generative AI cases.

rights_terms_note: Public federal agency page; quote sparingly; underlying complaint/case docket should be added separately for court-grade support.
cautious_summary: The EEOC stated that iTutorGroup programmed online recruitment software to automatically reject female applicants aged 55 or older and male applicants aged 60 or older, rejected more than 200 qualified U.S.-based applicants, and filed suit in federal court alleging age discrimination.
observatory_hook: Feeds workplace/HR enforcement matrix, algorithmic-discrimination case table, and regulator-to-court escalation view.

source_type: data protection authority enforcement news page
rights_terms_note: Public regulator page; no reuse-license review beyond ordinary citation performed this cycle.
cautious_summary: The ICO said it issued enforcement notices ordering Serco Leisure, Serco Jersey, and associated community leisure trusts to stop using facial recognition technology and fingerprint scanning to monitor worker attendance and to destroy biometric data not legally required to be retained.
observatory_hook: Feeds biometric-surveillance enforcement matrix, public-service provider oversight timeline, and workplace biometric proportionality figure.

source_type: data protection authority reprimand news page
rights_terms_note: Public regulator page; school and pupil context requires metadata-first treatment and no personal-detail expansion.
cautious_summary: The ICO said Chelmer Valley High School was reprimanded after introducing facial recognition technology for cashless canteen payments without first carrying out a DPIA and without properly obtaining explicit consent to process students' biometric information.
observatory_hook: Feeds children/school biometric governance matrix and dashboard filters for public-interest technology use involving minors.

rights_terms_note: Public federal agency page; summarizes settlement/decree terms from the agency perspective and should not be overstated as a full judicial merits finding.
cautious_summary: The EEOC stated that iTutorGroup would pay $365,000 to settle the discriminatory hiring suit and that the decree requires non-monetary relief including anti-discrimination policy changes, hiring-related training, injunctions against age- or sex-based discriminatory hiring and birth-date requests, and EEOC compliance monitoring if U.S. operations resume.
observatory_hook: Feeds regulator-to-settlement escalation matrix, HR enforcement lifecycle view, and contestability/remediation timeline for automated hiring cases.

rights_terms_note: Public regulator enforcement-action page; citation-safe metadata/details only; no bulk mirroring or notice-PDF capture performed this cycle.
cautious_summary: The ICO enforcement-action record classifies the Serco matter as enforcement notices in the transport and leisure sector and states that the regulator found unlawful processing of biometric data of more than 2,000 employees at 38 leisure facilities for attendance monitoring.
observatory_hook: Feeds regulator-action detail table, biometric workplace scale note, and regulator-to-public-record escalation matrix.

rights_terms_note: Public regulator enforcement-action page; pupil context kept metadata-first; note one-day date difference versus the linked newsroom story.
cautious_summary: The ICO enforcement-action record classifies the Chelmer Valley High School matter as a reprimand in the education and childcare sector and states that the school was reprimanded in respect of Article 35(1) for failing to complete a DPIA before introducing facial recognition for cashless catering.
observatory_hook: Feeds school-biometric governance matrix, public-record type facet, and regulator-to-public-record escalation matrix.

rights_terms_note: Public ICO PDF linked from the enforcement-action record; retain as citation metadata and short excerpts only.
cautious_summary: The ICO enforcement notice to Serco Leisure Operating Limited states that the Commissioner issued the notice under Data Protection Act 2018 section 149(2)(a) and (c) in relation to contraventions of Articles 5(1)(a), 6, and 9 UK GDPR, and Annex 1 requires Serco within three months to cease processing biometric data for employment attendance checks at relevant facilities and destroy biometric and other personal/special-category data not legally required to be retained.
observatory_hook: Feeds direct notice-text facet, biometric-workplace enforcement table, and regulator-action remedy-detail view for document-grade comparison.

rights_terms_note: Public ICO PDF linked from the enforcement-action record; keep use metadata-first and quote sparingly because the case concerns schoolchildren.
cautious_summary: The ICO reprimand to Chelmer Valley High School states that the Commissioner issued a reprimand under Article 58(2)(b) UK GDPR in respect of Article 35(1), records that the school introduced facial recognition for cashless catering in March 2023 without a prior DPIA, and states that from March to November 2023 the controller relied on assumed opt-out consent that would not have been valid or lawful consent for the processing.
observatory_hook: Feeds direct reprimand-text facet, school-biometric governance table, and minors/data-protection process-failure view for document-grade comparison.

observatory_hook: Feeds charge-stage court/enforcement facet, AI-washing versus hidden-human posture table, and regulator-to-court source-family saturation view.

rights_terms_note: Public SEC page; quote sparingly; use press-release framing only where the later administrative order is not the narrower or stronger source.
cautious_summary: The SEC announced settled charges against Delphia for making false and misleading statements about its purported use of AI and machine learning and said the firms in the batch agreed to pay civil penalties.
observatory_hook: Feeds posture-aware AI-washing matrix, regulator-final enforcement table, and finance-sector misstatement comparator view.

rights_terms_note: Public SEC PDF; settlement-order posture should be preserved as SEC findings accepted for settlement context, not independent judicial fact-finding.
cautious_summary: The SEC administrative order states that from at least August 2019 to August 2023 Delphia represented that it used artificial intelligence and machine learning to analyze retail clients' spending and social media data to inform investment advice when no such data was being used in its investment process, and orders a $225,000 civil money penalty.
observatory_hook: Feeds direct-order-text facet, settled AI-washing sanctions table, and reviewer-safe regulated-finance exemplar panel.

rights_terms_note: Public SEC page; shared batch press release duplicated here case-specifically so the authoritative ledger keeps per-case provenance.
cautious_summary: The SEC announced settled charges against Global Predictions for making false and misleading statements about its purported use of AI and said the firms in the batch agreed to pay civil penalties.
observatory_hook: Feeds posture-aware AI-washing matrix, regulator-final enforcement table, and finance-sector misstatement comparator view.

rights_terms_note: Public SEC PDF; settlement-order posture should remain explicit and should not be generalized into sector-wide prevalence or broader fraud findings.
cautious_summary: The SEC administrative order states that Global Predictions made false and misleading claims about its use of AI, including claims about expert AI-driven forecasts, first-regulated-AI-financial-advisor status, and service features, and orders a $175,000 civil money penalty.
observatory_hook: Feeds direct-order-text facet, settled AI-washing sanctions table, and reviewer-safe regulated-finance exemplar panel.

rights_terms_note: Public FCC document text; small single-document retrieval only; use as allegation-stage enforcement text and do not treat the apparent-liability findings as final adjudication.
cautious_summary: The FCC Notice of Apparent Liability proposes a $2,000,000 penalty against Lingo Telecom for applying incorrect STIR/SHAKEN attestations to spoofed robocalls carrying a deepfake generative-AI voice message purporting to be from the President of the United States and targeting New Hampshire primary voters.
observatory_hook: Feeds deepfake/synthetic-identity enforcement panel, telecom-authentication lifecycle table, election-interference authority slice, and authority-depth dashboard view.

rights_terms_note: Public FCC order text; small single-document retrieval only; use as disposition-stage settlement/order text and keep consent-decree posture explicit.
cautious_summary: The FCC Enforcement Bureau order states that Lingo Telecom entered into a Consent Decree to resolve the investigation, agreed to implement a robust compliance plan, and agreed to pay a $1,000,000 civil penalty in connection with STIR/SHAKEN attestation violations tied to the spoofed New Hampshire deepfake robocalls.
observatory_hook: Feeds deepfake/synthetic-identity enforcement panel, telecom-authentication lifecycle table, election-interference authority slice, and authority-depth dashboard view.

rights_terms_note: Public FCC release text; small single-document retrieval only; useful for public-facing wording, but the operative action remains the FCC order text.
cautious_summary: The FCC media release states that the settlement concerns spoofed robocalls using generative AI voice cloning technology to spread disinformation in connection with the New Hampshire presidential primary and describes the compliance plan as the first of its kind secured by the FCC.
observatory_hook: Feeds deepfake/synthetic-identity enforcement panel, telecom-authentication lifecycle table, election-interference authority slice, and authority-depth dashboard view.

issuing_body: Policija / Ministry of the Interior, Police
rights_terms_note: Public official police page; use metadata, short quotations, and bounded translation glosses only. No claim of independent legality, proportionality, effectiveness, or production deployment.
cautious_summary: The Slovenian police publicly host a Project ORION page stating that the project aims to improve border management with the help of artificial intelligence, describes the border-control approach as secure, reliable, and privacy-preserving, lists six sub-goals including interoperability with SIS/VIS/EES and ethical/legal compliance, and mentions privacy-preserving systems able to search and process encrypted biometric data plus pilot scenarios in Romania, Bulgaria, and Slovenia.
observatory_hook: Feeds jurisdiction/language map, public-sector AI deployment panel, policing/border-control topology, biometric/privacy tension matrix, and authority-depth geography diversification view.

rights_terms_note: Public FTC PDF; preserve FTC complaint posture and avoid unauthorized-practice or product-truth overread beyond the complaint/order text.
cautious_summary: The FTC complaint states that DoNotPay described its service as the world's first robot lawyer and an AI lawyer capable of drafting ironclad demand letters, contracts, and small-claims complaints, while alleging that the service was not designed to operate like a human lawyer in the respects advertised.
observatory_hook: nonfinance_official_ai_claim_anchor; posture-aware AI-washing panel; legal-service-substitution comparator

rights_terms_note: Public FTC PDF; preserve consent-order / final-order posture and do not generalize beyond the order's own prohibitions and monetary relief.
cautious_summary: The FTC final order bars DoNotPay from representing that a covered product or service operates like a human lawyer by applying relevant laws to subscribers' particular legal and factual situations and orders USD 193000 in monetary relief.
observatory_hook: nonfinance_official_ai_claim_anchor; authority-depth funnel; legal-service-substitution comparator

rights_terms_note: Public FTC PDF; preserve FTC complaint posture and avoid turning complaint allegations into a general scientific verdict on AI detectors.
cautious_summary: The FTC complaint states that Workado / Content at Scale AI claimed its AI Content Detector could predict with 98.3% accuracy whether text was generated using systems such as ChatGPT, GPT-4, Claude, and Bard, while alleging the model had not been tested for the plain-language text users typically submitted and that public results showed substantially lower performance than 98.3%.
observatory_hook: ai_detector_claims_anchor; false-capability matrix; education-research-integrity bridge

rights_terms_note: Public FTC PDF; preserve final-order posture and use only for the exact substantiation, recordkeeping, and customer-notice obligations in the order.
cautious_summary: The FTC final order bars Workado from making representations about an AI-detection product's effectiveness unless the representation is non-misleading and backed by competent and reliable evidence, and the order imposes preservation-of-testing-records and customer-notice obligations.
observatory_hook: ai_detector_claims_anchor; false-capability matrix; education-research-integrity bridge
